tweets.title:
India has now become the world’s fifth-largest military spender after its defence budget surged by 8.9 per cent.

According to a report released by the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) on Monday, global expenditure hit a record $2.89 trillion last year, and India stands in fifth position with a spending of $92.1 billion in 2025.
tweets.content:
India has now become the world’s fifth-largest military spender after its defence budget surged by 8.9 per cent.

According to a report released by the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) on Monday, global expenditure hit a record $2.89 trillion last year, and India stands in fifth position with a spending of $92.1 billion in 2025.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

GLOBAL DEFENCE SPENDING HITS 16-YEAR HIGH

A new report by Stockholm International Peace Research Institute shows global military spending surged to $2.9 trillion in 2025, the highest share of global GDP since 2009.

Highlights:
- Europe (+14%) and Asia (+8.1%) drove the spike
- Top spenders: U.S., China, Russia, Germany, India (58% of total)
- India spent ~$92.1B, up 8.9%

Rising tensions and ongoing conflicts are pushing nations toward long-term military expansion, with spending expected to keep climbing into 2026 and beyond.

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2048729053621043292#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 
27th April 2026  

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East 
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.
  
● Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi met with Russian President Putin in St. Petersburg today to discuss the conflict.

● Following the stall in US-Iran talks in Islamabad, Pakistan lifted security restrictions in the capital area.

● Iran offered, via Pakistani mediators, to end its restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the US lifting its naval blockade. Nuclear program discussions would be postponed to a later phase.

● Air India and IndiGo announced plans to resume flights from Qatar to India as regional airspace and operations gradually normalize.

● The fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire showed further strain, with continued Israeli strikes reported in southern Lebanon.

● Citing the vulnerability of chokepoints like Hormuz, the Thai government fast-tracked its $31 billion "Land Bridge" project today.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
27th April 2026

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.

● Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi met with Russian President Putin in St. Petersburg today to discuss the conflict.

● Following the stall in US-Iran talks in Islamabad, Pakistan lifted security restrictions in the capital area.

● Iran offered, via Pakistani mediators, to end its restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the US lifting its naval blockade. Nuclear program discussions would be postponed to a later phase.

● Air India and IndiGo announced plans to resume flights from Qatar to India as regional airspace and operations gradually normalize.

● The fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire showed further strain, with continued Israeli strikes reported in southern Lebanon.

● Citing the vulnerability of chokepoints like Hormuz, the Thai government fast-tracked its $31 billion "Land Bridge" project today.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
26th April 2026

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.

● Iran’s Foreign Minister Araghchi met Oman's Sultan in Muscat, discussing Strait of Hormuz security and broader regional efforts.

● Iran FM returns to Pakistan; phones Saudi & Egyptian counterparts.

● After Pakistan and Oman, Araghchi is scheduled to meet Russian leaders in Moscow tomorrow for consultations on Iran-US negotiations.

● Iranian MP Ebrahim Rezaei previously questioned Pakistan’s role as a fully neutral mediator.

● Kuwait International Airport resumed limited commercial operations.

● Israel reports strikes on Hezbollah facilities in Lebanon.

● India’s NSA Ajit Doval meets UAE President in Abu Dhabi on West Asia situation.

● A fire broke out in a storage building at the UK base used by the US Air Force for Iran-related operations. No injuries or aircraft damage reported; cause believed accidental.

● An armed suspect opened fire while attempting to breach security at the event in Washington, where Trump was present. Trump was unharmed; the suspect was apprehended by Secret Service.

END OF THE BRIEF

They have a provision that MPs accused of corruption or money laundering will not be suspended from Parliament unless they are in judical custoday.

Certainly a person of interest for India.

tweets.title:
GLOBAL DEFENCE SPENDING HITS 16-YEAR HIGH

A new report by Stockholm International Peace Research Institute shows global military spending surged to $2.9 trillion in 2025, the highest share of global GDP since 2009.

Highlights:
- Europe (+14%) and Asia (+8.1%) drove the spike
- Top spenders: U.S., China, Russia, Germany, India (58% of total)
- India spent ~$92.1B, up 8.9%

Rising tensions and ongoing conflicts are pushing nations toward long-term military expansion, with spending expected to keep climbing into 2026 and beyond.
tweets.content:
GLOBAL DEFENCE SPENDING HITS 16-YEAR HIGH

A new report by Stockholm International Peace Research Institute shows global military spending surged to $2.9 trillion in 2025, the highest share of global GDP since 2009.

Highlights:
- Europe (+14%) and Asia (+8.1%) drove the spike
- Top spenders: U.S., China, Russia, Germany, India (58% of total)
- India spent ~$92.1B, up 8.9%

Rising tensions and ongoing conflicts are pushing nations toward long-term military expansion, with spending expected to keep climbing into 2026 and beyond.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: The action against Jamia Siraj-ul-Uloom, Imam Sahib, Shopian, has not been taken arbitrarily but is based on sustained inputs and investigation. Over the years, multiple linkages have emerged connecting individuals associated with the institution to terrorist networks, including Jaish-e-Mohammed. As per available records, a 11 former students have joined terrorist ranks, and several staff members have been detained for acting as OGWs. Notably, Sajad Ahmad Bhat who joined JeM shortly after the Pulwama attack, was also former student of this institute. The explosive laden Maruti Eeco used in the attack was owned by Sajad.  Such instances raise serious concerns regarding the ecosystem and influence surrounding the institution. While the contribution of any educational institute is acknowledged, it cannot be overlooked if there is credible evidence of misuse of its platform, premises, or network for unlawful and anti-national activities. National security and public safety remain paramount.

The designation is a legal measure based on evidence. Any challenge to it may be pursued through due process of law, but it is incorrect to portray such action as arbitrary or politically motivated.
tweets.content:

tweets.title:
Iran Sends New Peace Proposal to U.S. via Pakistan

Iran has reportedly submitted a “new proposal” to the United States through Pakistani intermediaries, aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, according to reports.

 Key points of the proposal:
- Two-stage plan; first restore maritime flow & ease blockade
- Option of extended ceasefire or permanent end to conflict
- Nuclear talks to be deferred until after Hormuz is reopened

Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reportedly told mediators there is no internal consensus yet on U.S. demands like halting uranium enrichment.
tweets.content:
Iran Sends New Peace Proposal to U.S. via Pakistan

Iran has reportedly submitted a “new proposal” to the United States through Pakistani intermediaries, aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, according to reports.

Key points of the proposal:
- Two-stage plan; first restore maritime flow & ease blockade
- Option of extended ceasefire or permanent end to conflict
- Nuclear talks to be deferred until after Hormuz is reopened

Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reportedly told mediators there is no internal consensus yet on U.S. demands like halting uranium enrichment.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
25th April 2026

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.

● Iranian FM Araghchi began his meetings with Pakistani officials today. He emphasized that his tour is focused on a "neighborhood first" policy with partners in Pakistan, Oman, and Russia.

● US officials Witkoff and Kushner arrive in Islamabad, however Iran denies any talks with US.

● UKMTO reported the hijacking of a tanker approximately 45nm northeast of Mareeyo, Somalia.

● Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ warned that if US continues its naval blockade and maritime piracy in the region, it will encounter a severe reaction from Iran’s armed forces.

● Israeli PM Netanyahu is expected to travel to Washington in about two and a half weeks for the summit with President Trump and Lebanese President Aoun.

● Iran resumes international services of Imam Khomeini Airport.

● Despite the ceasefire extension, the IDF struck Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon today, killing at least four people.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title:
DRDO Unveils Next-Gen Indigenous Armoured Platforms

Defence Research and Development Organisation has flagged off advanced tracked and wheeled armoured platforms at VRDE, Ahilyanagar. Built with ~65% indigenous content (target 90%), manufactured by Tata Advanced Systems Limited and Bharat Forge Limited.

Highlights:
- Designed for Infantry Combat Vehicle (ICV) & APC roles
- Equipped with 30mm crewless turret + ATGM capability
- STANAG Level 4–5 protection with modular armour
- Amphibious capability with hydro jets
tweets.content:
DRDO Unveils Next-Gen Indigenous Armoured Platforms

Defence Research and Development Organisation has flagged off advanced tracked and wheeled armoured platforms at VRDE, Ahilyanagar. Built with ~65% indigenous content (target 90%), manufactured by Tata Advanced Systems Limited and Bharat Forge Limited.

Highlights:
- Designed for Infantry Combat Vehicle (ICV) & APC roles
- Equipped with 30mm crewless turret + ATGM capability
- STANAG Level 4–5 protection with modular armour
- Amphibious capability with hydro jets

tweets.title:
Shots were fired during a White House press dinner, triggering chaos as Donald Trump was rushed to safety by Secret Service.
Authorities say the heavily armed suspect was subdued and arrested, with Trump describing him as a possible “lone wolf”, though investigations are ongoing.

At least one security officer was injured but survived due to protective gear. The attacker reportedly breached security and charged toward the venue before being stopped.
tweets.content:

At least one security officer was injured but survived due to protective gear. The attacker reportedly breached security and charged toward the venue before being stopped.
